mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in over 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which pushes corporate earnings higher and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window, or positive returns drop below 55% when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening economic activity and lower corporate revenue expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during monetary tightening cycles.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of tightening cycle observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 325, inflationary pressures force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in over 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 325, or the threshold loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates healthy job creation without labor market slack, supporting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns fall below 50% when payrolls rise and unemployment stays flat.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects strong economic growth and higher corporate profit margins, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of expanding GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the industrial production index below 100 signals a contraction in manufacturing and industrial output, reducing aggregate demand and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO falls below 100, with positive 60-day returns exceeding 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains insignificant.
